how_to_approve: Review every rule and rationale. To approve, re-run with approvals={"rules": {"approver": "<your name>", "sha256": "3bc2b42b5515f2a04e7df453c9462caa807d19264fa29fae79ecd8520a45da9a"}}. The hash binds the approval to exactly this draft.
rationales: 'invoice_id' has zero nulls across 300 rows - the profile suggests completeness is an expectation, so future nulls should be exceptions.
'invoice_id' is distinct on 300 of 300 rows (ratio >= 0.999) - it behaves as an identifier, so duplicates should be exceptions.
'vendor' has zero nulls across 300 rows - the profile suggests completeness is an expectation, so future nulls should be exceptions.

'amount' is distinct on 300 of 300 rows (ratio >= 0.999) - it behaves as an identifier, so duplicates should be exceptions.
'issued_at' has zero nulls across 300 rows - the profile suggests completeness is an expectation, so future nulls should be exceptions.
'issued_at' is a TIMESTAMP column - future-dated records are a validity finding in most operational data.
